mnemonics: FINITE VS. INFINITE: Finite sets are countable like counting fingers, infinite sets like natural numbers are also countable if you can list them one-by-one, but real numbers are uncountable - there's no list long enough!
keyPoints: A set is countable if its elements can be put into a one-to-one correspondence with the natural numbers (finite sets are also considered countable)
The set of real numbers is uncountable, proven by Cantor's diagonal argument
The rational numbers are countable despite being dense, while the irrational numbers are uncountable
commonMistakes: Assuming all infinite sets are the same size (some infinities are larger than others)
Thinking a set is uncountable just because it's infinite (e.g., rational numbers are infinite but countable)
Confusing density with cardinality (a set can be dense in R but still countable like Q)
